title: Admin Executive (Japanese MNC/Entry Level)
description:
Job Summary

Manage administrative and operational support functions to enhance office efficiency and support senior management, including handling communications, vendor coordination, travel arrangements, and corporate event logistics.

Responsibilities

  • Manage incoming calls and direct them promptly to the intended recipients to ensure efficient communication flow
  • Offer and serve beverages to management and guests, enhancing hospitality standards
  • Collect and distribute incoming mails to maintain timely correspondence
  • Handle and coordinate courier services to support office logistics
  • Perform general administrative duties including monitoring and replenishing pantry items, stationery, name card requests, and other office supplies to maintain operational readiness
  • Liaise with contractors and vendors to coordinate office maintenance and services
  • Order office supplies, maintain inventory records, and track office expenses to ensure budget compliance
  • Arrange travel and accommodation bookings to support business travel needs
  • Prepare invitation letters for visa arrangements to facilitate international travel
  • Coordinate data roaming and SIM card arrangements for mobile connectivity needs
  • Provide administrative support to the Managing Director to streamline executive operations
  • Support expatriate staff with housing, memberships, maintenance follow-up, and apartment handover to ensure smooth relocation and accommodation management
  • Distribute monthly Public Holiday Notices to NOC offices to maintain compliance with local schedules
  • File confidential documents such as Rental Agreements and Distributorship Agreements with strict adherence to confidentiality protocols
  • Support corporate event logistics to ensure successful event execution
  • Assist with audit checkpoints to maintain compliance and readiness
  • Conduct monthly 5S desk checks and asset management to uphold workplace organization standards
  • Manage quarterly carpark season matters to coordinate parking allocations
  • Oversee rental of vehicles and petrol card management to support transportation needs
  • Manage purchase of vouchers for the sales department to facilitate incentives and rewards
  • Coordinate staff pass issuance as required to ensure access control
  • Manage uniform distribution and inventory for the Service department to maintain professional appearance
  • Process contract tea lady’s claims and leave applications on Coupa/HRIQ to ensure accurate payroll and attendance records
  • Perform additional duties assigned by management to support evolving business needs

Required competencies and certifications

  • Minimum Diploma education in any relevant area
  • Demonstrated proficiency in MS Office and other IT software

Preferred competencies and qualifications

  • 1 to 2 years’ experience in a similar administrative role

postedCompany.description:
Singapore's leading company to provide SME consultations. 


With more than 10 years of collective consulting experience, we provide personalized solutions for our corporate clients, ranging from multinational corporations to the public sector as well as small and medium enterprises.
